title: Eurosystem balance sheet
summary: This source is a container page on the ECB website that provides links to download annual consolidated balance sheet data for the Eurosystem (1999-2025) and weekly financial statements. The page does not contain actual balance sheet figures, analytical commentary, or substantive research content. It references a consolidated balance sheet as of 31 December 2025 published 26 February 2026, and Annual Accounts 2025 also published 26 February 2026. Changes to presentation format were made in line with Guideline (EU) 2024/2941.
key_points: Eurosystem annual consolidated balance sheet comprises assets and liabilities of NCBs and ECB at year-end
Intra-Eurosystem claims and liabilities are netted and not shown
Data available for download: Annual consolidated balance sheet 1999-2025 (CSV and XLS formats)
Weekly financial statements published 26 February 2026 for consolidated balance sheet as at 31 December 2025
why_it_matters: The Eurosystem balance sheet provides aggregate data on central bank assets and liabilities across the euro area. However, this source page does not contain the actual numerical data—it only provides download links to CSV and XLS files. The raw data files would be needed for any substantive macro analysis.
macro_context: Central bank balance sheet data is a key macro indicator for assessing monetary policy stance, liquidity conditions, and potential QE or QT programs. The 2025 year-end balance sheet data (referenced but not present in this source) would be relevant for analyzing ECB policy trajectory.

issues_found: Source is a container/listing page with no substantive analytical content
No actual balance sheet figures or numerical data present in source body
Source only contains links to downloadable CSV/XLS files
No URL provided in source metadata - cannot verify or revisit source
Cannot assess market impact or macro relevance without actual data
Archive value is too low for standalone research record

notes: Low-quality source: container page without substantive content. The actual Eurosystem balance sheet data exists in downloadable CSV/XLS files but is not present in this source text. Archive value is limited until actual data files are retrieved and processed.
